meta.title: Stigmergy: Leviathan Through the Elixir Lens
meta.description: OTP Supervision Trees, Graph-Addressed Mail, and the Missing Memory Layer — mapping Leviathan's core modules to Erlang/OTP primitives.

props.title: Stigmergy: Leviathan Through the Elixir Lens
props.subtitle: OTP Supervision Trees, Graph-Addressed Mail, and the Missing Memory Layer

props.content: Leviathan's core/ modules map almost 1:1 onto Erlang/OTP primitives — GenServers, Supervisors, Registries, PubSub. This page makes the mapping explicit, reveals the memory layer that completes the picture, and charts the path from today's levmail to tomorrow's graph-addressed bulletin system.

props.label: MODULE TAXONOMY
props.title: The Elixir Mapping
props.subtitle: Every core/ module has an OTP counterpart. Here's the Rosetta Stone.

props.title: 🔷 graph → GenServer + Registry + PubSub
props.content: Holds entity state, dispatches events, registers flows. The FlowRegistry bridges GenServer state management with PubSub event distribution.

props.title: ⚙️ flowmind → Application + Compiler
props.content: Loads YAML IR, compiles to executable graph operations, manages sessions. The Application behavior spec that boots the system's brain.

props.title: 🔀 poly → Registry + Supervisor dispatch
props.content: Named handler lookup, surface routing (CLI / MCP / HTTP / gRPC / WS). **RENAME CANDIDATE** — it's really the handler registry + surface multiplexer.

props.title: 🎯 orchestration → Supervisor + Task + GenStage
props.content: DAG waves, queues, execution contracts, loop machinery. The heavy-duty process orchestration layer.

props.title: 📐 config → Application.get_env
props.content: Fractal cascading config resolution. Reads from DNA, project, user, and global scopes — just like `Application.get_env` with nested fallback.

props.title: 🧪 harness → Supervisor + DynamicSupervisor
props.content: Process lifecycle, agent sessions, multi-provider dispatch. DynamicSupervisor because agent sessions are started and stopped at runtime.

props.title: 🧠 memory → Registry + GenServer facade
props.content: Backend registry, hybrid orchestrator. Routes to the right memory backend (episodic, procedural, semantic) through a unified GenServer facade.

props.title: 📡 event-bus → Phoenix.PubSub
props.content: Event routing. Topic-based publish/subscribe with the same semantics as Phoenix.PubSub — fire, subscribe, broadcast.

props.title: 🤖 afk → Supervisor with max_restarts kill gates
props.content: Autonomous run supervision. Like a Supervisor that counts restarts and kills the child permanently when it crosses the threshold.

props.title: 🌳 daemon → Application / Supervisor root
props.content: Daemon lifecycle, startup tree. The root of the supervision tree — `Application.start` that boots every other process.

props.label: MEMORY ARCHITECTURE
props.title: Stigmergy: The Missing Memory Layer
props.subtitle: Four memory types complete the cognitive stack. Three exist. One is designed and ready to build.

rows.stores: What agents learned about a region

props.label: NODE ARCHITECTURE
props.title: The Rich Node Model
props.subtitle: Each graph node accumulates facets over time. Here's what sticks — and who reads it.

props.title: DNA
props.content: Constraint definitions, behaviors, failure modes, validation rules. Written by architects, read by every runtime process.

props.title: Config
props.content: Resolved configuration values from fractal cascade. Written by config resolver, read by handlers and orchestration.

props.title: Hooks
props.content: Lifecycle callbacks — pre/post execution, validation gates, side-effect triggers. Written by plugin authors, read by harness.

props.title: Exec History
props.content: Run records, pass/fail, timing, resource usage. Written by orchestration, read by FlowMind and dashboards.

props.title: Agent Trace
props.content: Which agent touched this node, when, what it did. Written by harness sessions, read by coordination and audit.

props.title: Bulletins
props.content: Graph-addressed mail with TTL decay and cascade radius. Written by any agent, read by agents traversing the region. **NEW.**

props.title: Manifestation
props.content: Final rendered output — the artifact this node produces. Written by surface renderers, read by users and downstream consumers.

props.label: MAIL ROUTING
props.title: Three Addressing Modes
props.subtitle: From point-to-point to topological proximity — three ways to route information.

props.title: 📬 Direct
props.content: **to: agent://A3** — Point-to-point delivery. Only A3 sees it. Standard levmail behavior today.

props.title: 📢 Broadcast
props.content: **to: graph://* cascade: inf** — Everyone sees it. Current all-agents broadcast. No topology awareness.

props.title: 📌 Bulletin
props.content: **to: graph://<node> cascade: N** — Topological proximity with bounded spread. Agents within N hops encounter the message. **NEW.**

props.label: DNA ↔ OTP
props.title: DNA as Supervision Specs
props.subtitle: DNA constraint files map directly to OTP child_spec fields — fault tolerance, observability, and restart policy.

props.label: TOPOLOGY
props.title: FlowMind Zone Heat Map
props.subtitle: Bulletin density and agent activity create four zone types across the graph.

props.title: 🔴 Hot Zone
props.content: Many recent bulletins, multiple agents active. **Action:** Coordinate before touching — high collision risk.

props.title: 🔵 Cold Zone
props.content: No bulletins, no claims. The output of `findGaps()`. **Action:** Unexplored territory — safe to claim and start fresh.

props.title: ⚡ Conflict Zone
props.content: Contradictory bulletins on adjacent nodes. **Action:** Route to CDO / architecture review before proceeding.

props.title: 🟢 Settled Zone
props.content: Old bulletins, consistent, low activity. **Action:** Stable ground — safe to build on without coordination overhead.

props.label: NAMING
props.title: Naming Consolidation Candidates
props.subtitle: Known naming inconsistencies across the codebase. Options listed, not decided.

rows.rationale: It's the handler registry + surface multiplexer

rows.problem: Overloaded — means intermediate representation AND architecture review
rows.options: Drop IR for arch, keep for compiler

props.label: EXECUTION
props.title: What's Left — Runtime Joins
props.subtitle: Four P0 tasks to full integration. All status: READY.

props.title: S1: Agent Smoke Test
props.content: 13 MCP tools exercised end-to-end. Validates that the handler registry resolves every registered tool. **Status: READY**

props.title: S2: Integration + Harness
props.content: Multi-provider harness sessions, lifecycle hooks, and error propagation. **Status: READY**

props.title: S3: FlowMind Graph-Ops
props.content: FlowMind sessions compile to graph operations. Verify the IR → graph-op pipeline is lossless. **Status: READY**

props.title: S4: EventBridge Wiring
props.content: Daemon boots the event bus, all modules subscribe at startup. The root of the supervision tree comes alive. **Status: READY**

props.label: UPGRADE PATH
props.title: levmail Upgrade Path
props.subtitle: Three layers — from zero-code-change adoption to full graph-backed resolution.

props.title: Layer 1 — Zero Code Change
props.content: Use **graph://** strings in the `to:` field now. The file format already holds it. Agents and humans can start writing bulletin-addressed mail today with no tooling changes.

props.title: Layer 2 — ~30 Lines Bash
props.content: `levmail bulletin <node> --cascade 2 --ttl 48h` — Validates the `graph://` prefix, adds frontmatter fields (cascade, TTL, posted-at). A thin wrapper, not a rewrite.

props.title: Layer 3 — Stigmergy
props.content: Full graph-backed resolution: cascade hops, agent-claim registry, TTL decay, zone heat maps. Depends on S4 EventBridge wiring. The complete topological memory system. [Design doc →](https://github.com/jean-patricksmith/digital-leviathan/blob/main/docs/design/design-graph-addressed-mail.md)

props.label: PRIOR ART
props.title: Precedent
props.subtitle: Four decades of indirect coordination research that converge here.

props.title: 🐜 Stigmergy (Grassé, 1959)
props.content: Ant pheromone trails. Indirect coordination through environmental modification. Agents don't talk to each other — they modify the shared environment and others react. [Read →](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stigmergy)

props.title: 📋 Blackboard Architecture (Hayes-Roth, 1985)
props.content: Shared workspace, independent knowledge sources post partial solutions. No direct communication — all coordination flows through the board. [Read →](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blackboard_(design_pattern))

props.title: 📦 Tuple Spaces (Gelernter, 1985)
props.content: Linda coordination language. Processes communicate by writing and reading tuples in a shared associative space. Decoupled in time and space. [Read →](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tuple_space)

props.title: ⚡ Elixir/OTP (Erlang, 1986+)
props.content: Lightweight processes, message passing, supervision trees, let-it-crash philosophy. The production-proven realization of actor-model fault tolerance. [Read →](https://elixir-lang.org/getting-started/mix-otp/supervisor-and-application.html)
